author: y4rr
content:
Landed GPU \`masked_sum\` / \`masked_mean\`; min/max split to #616.

## What landed in \`ferrotorch-core/src/masked.rs\`

### \`MaskedTensor::new\` accepts GPU tensors
Removed the eager \`NotImplementedOnCuda\` rejection. Both CPU and CUDA data tensors are now valid construction inputs. The numel/shape check is unchanged.

### \`masked_sum_gpu\` lowering
\`\`\`rust
fn masked_sum_gpu<T: Float>(mt: &MaskedTensor<T>) -> FerrotorchResult<Tensor<T>> {
    let mask_t: Tensor<T> = mask_as_float_tensor(&mt.mask, mt.data.shape(), device)?;
    let prod_h = backend.mul_f32(data, mask_t);  // or mul_f64
    let sum_h = backend.sum_f32(&prod_h, numel);  // or sum_f64
    Tensor::from_storage(TensorStorage::gpu(sum_h), vec![], false)
}
\`\`\`
Builds a float-valued mask tensor (true → 1.0, false → 0.0), uploads to the same device as the data tensor, then chains existing \`mul + reduce_sum\` trait methods. No new GPU kernels required — the lowering is pure dispatch.

### \`masked_mean_gpu\` lowering
\`masked_sum_gpu\` divided by \`count_valid\` (computed on host from the bool mask — constant-time, avoids an extra GPU round-trip). All-masked case short-circuits to NaN.

### Helpers
- \`mask_as_float_tensor(mask, shape, device)\` — builds the 0/1 float tensor and routes to the right device
- \`is_f32::<T>()\` / \`is_f64::<T>()\` — local versions of the dtype-detection helpers used elsewhere

## Tests (5 new in \`test_gpu_masked.rs\`)
- \`masked_sum_f32_matches_cpu\` — non-trivial mask, end-to-end vs CPU
- \`masked_sum_f64_matches_cpu\` — same for f64
- \`masked_sum_all_masked_returns_zero\` — all-false mask path
- \`masked_mean_f32_matches_cpu\` — partial mask
- \`masked_mean_all_masked_is_nan\` — short-circuit path

5 GPU masked tests pass. \`cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --features cuda\`: zero regressions.

## Out of scope → #616
\`masked_min\` / \`masked_max\` need new \`reduce_min\` / \`reduce_max\` PTX kernels (the existing \`reduce_sum\` is the model — same two-pass block reduction with op + identity swapped). Lowering is straightforward once the kernels exist: \`reduce_min(where(mask, data, +∞))\`, with host short-circuit on \`count_valid == 0\`.
